role_title: Strategic Operations Lead, User Safety & Risk Operations

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in operations, strategy and operations, product operations, technical program management, trust and safety operations, risk operations, implementation or management consulting, or a related field
Explicitly described as a senior IC role

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in operations, strategy and operations, product operations, technical program management, trust and safety operations, risk operations, implementation or management consulting, or a related field.
Track record of leading complex, ambiguous work from problem framing through execution.
Strong technical fluency, including the ability to reason through data flows, system ownership, logs, dashboards, tooling gaps, and failure modes.
Strong data literacy, including experience using SQL, spreadsheets, dashboards, and metrics to analyze workflows, assess risk, identify gaps, and drive decision making.
Able to work closely with Product, Engineering, Data Science, and technical operations teams; ask the right questions; and translate technical constraints into operational requirements.
AI-fluent, with a track record of using AI tools to move faster, improve quality, or create operational leverage.
Comfortable operating where data access, tooling, ownership, or policy thresholds are incomplete or changing.
Implementation-oriented and comfortable building the workflow, not just defining the strategy.

Tool-agnostic and resourceful, with a track record of quickly learning unfamiliar systems and building effective solutions with limited resources, including lightweight tools, AI-assisted workflows, dashboards, documentation, and process design.
Strong interpersonal judgment, with the ability to build trust across teams while keeping ownership, accountability, and escalation paths clear.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience supporting cloud, enterprise, infrastructure, platform, or partner-hosted deployments.
Experience building launch-readiness or post-launch monitoring processes for complex technical programs.
Experience designing workflows that include escalation paths, QA, capacity planning, reporting, and long-term ownership transitions.
Experience in implementation-focused consulting, product operations, technical program management, or strategy and operations roles where success depended on execution, not only planning.

Experience prototyping lightweight tools, automations, dashboards, or workflow improvements using AI-assisted development tools such as Codex, low-code/no-code platforms, SQL, spreadsheets, or scripting.

scope.surface: User Safety & Risk Operations (USRO) — new team focused on building operational capacity for new, ambiguous, or fast-moving safety and risk areas
scope.business_model: internal operations / platform safety for AI research and deployment company
primary_metrics_inferred: Escalation path clarity and resolution rate
Workflow health, capacity, and quality gaps
core_responsibilities: Lead strategic operational builds from ambiguous problem statement to implemented operating model
Build launch and early-life operating models for complex partner deployments
Translate technical, policy, safety, privacy, and operational constraints into practical workflows
Define how safety and risk signals should be monitored, reviewed, packaged, escalated, and tracked through resolution
Partner with senior leaders to translate needs into clear execution plans
Use data and operational signals to understand workflow health, capacity needs, bottlenecks, quality gaps, and risks
Identify where AI tools, automation, lightweight systems, or better workflows can reduce operational burden
Keep operating models current as data pipelines, tooling, dashboards, partner processes, and ownership mature

green_flags: Clearly scoped role with well-articulated responsibilities and context
Senior IC role with direct impact on high-visibility safety programs at OpenAI
Encourages applications from diverse backgrounds (ops, consulting, TPM, trust & safety)
Hybrid policy explicitly stated (3 days/week in-office, San Francisco)
red_flags: Role requires 3 days/week in-office in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
High ambiguity environment may be challenging for candidates without prior trust & safety or risk operations experience
8+ years minimum is a hard floor for a broad set of qualifying fields — may be inconsistently applied

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from '8+ years' requirement and 'senior IC role' explicit label in body)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not directly stated; inferred from responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and team context)
ambiguities: The 'You Might Thrive in This Role If You' section header is softer phrasing than 'Required', but the items listed are clearly baseline expectations for a senior IC hire (e.g., 8+ years, SQL, technical fluency). Treated as must_haves with severity: hard based on context and unconditional bullet structure.
No compensation range disclosed — could not assess seniority by comp signal.
